# Clawq

> Clawq is a formally verified personal AI assistant runtime — Coq-proven core properties extracted to OCaml, with multi-channel integrations, sandboxed built-in tools, MCP support, audit logging, a streaming web UI, restart/resume behavior, and self-hosted gateway/channel operation from `~/.clawq/config.json`. For full self-knowledge including every CLI command, config field, tool, channel setup guide, gateway route, and troubleshooting detail, see the full context file.

## Docs

- [Full self-knowledge context](https://clawq.org/llms-full.txt): Complete self-reference with every CLI command, config section and field with defaults, all built-in tools, channel setup walkthroughs, gateway endpoints, restart/resume behavior, first-time setup guide, and troubleshooting
- [Quick Start](https://clawq.org/quickstart): Install from npm, configure a provider, open the web UI, add a channel — 5 minutes
- [Configuration](https://clawq.org/configuration): Full config reference — providers, channels, security, gateway, memory, resilience, MCP, tunnels
- [CLI Reference](https://clawq.org/cli-reference): Complete command reference for all clawq commands and subcommands
- [Channels](https://clawq.org/channels): Telegram, Discord, Slack, GitHub, MS Teams setup and channel startup registry
- [Architecture](https://clawq.org/architecture): Coq-to-OCaml build pipeline, runtime module map, library dependency structure
- [Tools](https://clawq.org/tools): Built-in tools reference, MCP server/client, skills overview, sandbox backends, shell allowlist
- [Background Tasks](https://clawq.org/background-tasks): Delegate coding tasks to background agent runners with git worktree isolation
- [Skills](https://clawq.org/skills): SKILL.md definitions, discovery paths, @mention and /slash syntax, custom skill walkthrough
- [Security](https://clawq.org/security): Security model, dual path safety, sandbox backends, Landlock, secret encryption, audit log, rate limiting

## Integrations

- [MS Teams](https://clawq.org/integrations/teams/llms.txt): MS Teams Bot Framework channel setup, config reference, auth details, and troubleshooting

## Tools

- [Chat Log Viewer](https://clawq.org/chat-viewer): Drop-in browser viewer for clawq session show and Claude Code JSON chat logs — renders messages, tool calls, thinking blocks

## Optional

- [Development Guide](https://clawq.org/development): Build, test, format, extraction workflow, code style, contributing
- [Verification Status](https://clawq.org/formal-verification): Machine-checked Coq verification status across 22 phases (374 theorems)
- [Verification Audit](https://clawq.org/formal-verification-audit-2026-03-07): Detailed audit of verified properties
- [Verification Roadmap](https://clawq.org/formal-verification-roadmap-2026-03-07): Planned verification expansions
- [Verification Boundaries](https://clawq.org/verification-boundaries): Cross-cutting view of verification status across all security-relevant subsystems (scope resolution, memory policy, credentials, egress, MCP filtering, sessions)
- [Scope Resolution Invariants](https://clawq.org/scope-resolution-invariants): Invariants for the scope resolver and access snapshots — determinism, precedence, conflict resolution, global security caps, repo/memory/channel grants, reload and backward compat
- [Memory Policy Isolation Invariants](https://clawq.org/memory-policy-isolation-invariants): Invariants for scoped memory CRUD, visibility levels, grant resolution, credential non-disclosure, egress default-deny, MCP filter soundness, budget, session lifecycle, redaction, ledger audit trail
- [Proof Backlog](https://clawq.org/proof-backlog): Catalogue of invariants that are candidates for formal proof or stronger verification across scope resolution and memory policy subsystems
- [Pilot Setup Wizard](https://clawq.org/pilot-setup-wizard): Interactive and non-interactive room-agent profile configuration with plan/apply/rerun modes, Teams-first rollout
- [Teams-First Room-Agent UX](https://clawq.org/teams-first-room-agent-ux): Room-origin background task delivery through chat connectors — progress cards, Adaptive Cards, delivery lifecycle, capability introspection, Slack baseline
